summary:
The primary nerve supplying sensory function at the angle of the jaw includes branches derived mainly from the mandibular division (V3) of the trigeminal nerve; however, it does so through its own specialized distribution rather than via nearby cervical or cranial nerves which have more limited involvement here.

Agent1's reasoning (Round 2): After re-evaluating my response with an emphasis solely on clarity without extraneous details:

The main supplier of neural impulses to the angle of the jaw comes directly from branchlets originating within the mandibular division (V3) of the trigeminal nerve due to their precise anatomical relationship there. No significant contribution arises predominantly from adjacent peripheral nerves such as the lesser occipital or great auricular since these serve different regional needs instead.
Thus,
Summary: Sensory input to the angle of the jaw originates principally from branches of the mandibular division (V3) of the trigeminal nerve, distinctively serving this localized area compared to neighboring superficial nerves whose functions lie elsewhere.
